title: TSP-Relaxed Reasoning Path Optimization for Large Reasoning Models: A Continuous Combinatorial Framework for End-to-End Inference Path Learning
author: Zhang, Jincheng
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The reasoning process of a large-scale reasoning model (LRM) can be viewed as an ordered path generation problem consisting of multiple intermediate reasoning states. However, existing methods typically treat reasoning as a discrete token sequence optimization problem, ignoring the global structural dependencies and path optimality issues between reasoning steps, leading to redundant reasoning and suboptimal path expansion. This paper proposes a novel training/tuning paradigm: the reasoning path optimization framework TRRPO based on the relaxation theory of the Traveling Salesman Problem (TSP). This method treats reasoning steps as "nodes" in a high-dimensional semantic space, and the complete reasoning chain as an optimal access path. It transforms the optimization of discrete reasoning sequences into a differentiable global path optimization problem through continuous relaxation. We construct a semantic cost matrix between reasoning steps and introduce a "soft permutation matrix" to continuously represent the discrete reasoning sequence, enabling the model to learn the "shortest effective reasoning path" during training while automatically suppressing redundant reasoning nodes. Furthermore, we propose a path compression objective based on the relaxed TSP energy function, allowing the model to minimize reasoning path length and semantic redundancy while ensuring answer correctness. This paradigm does not rely on external rule pruning, but instead learns inference termination and path pruning mechanisms intrinsically through a global optimization structure, thereby achieving a unified optimization of inference efficiency and quality.
steps_raw: The reasoning process of a large-scale reasoning model (LRM) can be viewed as an ordered path generation problem consisting of multiple intermediate reasoning states. However, existing methods typically treat reasoning as a discrete token sequence optimization problem, ignoring the global structural dependencies and path optimality issues between reasoning steps, leading to redundant reasoning and suboptimal path expansion. This paper proposes a novel training/tuning paradigm: the reasoning path optimization framework TRRPO based on the relaxation theory of the Traveling Salesman Problem (TSP). This method treats reasoning steps as "nodes" in a high-dimensional semantic space, and the complete reasoning chain as an optimal access path. It transforms the optimization of discrete reasoning sequences into a differentiable global path optimization problem through continuous relaxation. We construct a semantic cost matrix between reasoning steps and introduce a "soft permutation matrix" to continuously represent the discrete reasoning sequence, enabling the model to learn the "shortest effective reasoning path" during training while automatically suppressing redundant reasoning nodes. Furthermore, we propose a path compression objective based on the relaxed TSP energy function, allowing the model to minimize reasoning path length and semantic redundancy while ensuring answer correctness. This paradigm does not rely on external rule pruning, but instead learns inference termination and path pruning mechanisms intrinsically through a global optimization structure, thereby achieving a unified optimization of inference efficiency and quality.
